title: The Bost-Connes Modular Generator as a candidate Hilbert-Pólya Operator, formalized in Lean 4
description:
Lean 4 formalization companion to the paper "The Bost-Connes Modular Generator as a candidate Hilbert-Pólya Operator".

This repository implements an operator-algebraic construction of a candidate Hilbert-Pólya operator D_∞ from the Bost-Connes type III_1 factor's modular data, with surrounding Galerkin approximation chain (Bögli-Siegl-Tretter spectral exactness, Hurwitz zero convergence, Rellich-Kondrachov discrete compactness), crystallizing the Riemann Hypothesis as a single concrete type-inhabitation problem.

The canonical terminal rh_of_ccm_galerkin (g : CCMGalerkinSpectralData) : RiemannHypothesis is a TRANSPARENT CONDITIONAL on the named Galerkin spectral-data gate. Inhabiting CCMGalerkinSpectralData is equivalent to the Riemann Hypothesis. NO term of CCMGalerkinSpectralData is constructed in this formalization; the spectral encoding is DERIVED from the gate fields via genuine Bögli/Hurwitz/Anselone/Rouché machinery. This is NOT an unconditional closure of the Riemann Hypothesis.

Mathlib pinned at SHA 5e932f97 (Lean 4 v4.29.1). Lake-dep on tt-bost-connes-lean v0.2 (Tomita-Takesaki modular substrate; DOI 10.5281/zenodo.20674891). The chain is verified with zero sorry count and 8 named programme-level axioms (2 on the canonical terminal closure — Rouché 1862 and Anselone 1971 / Stummel 1970; 6 off-terminal background substrate) plus 6 TT-upstream-inherited axioms (off-terminal; lake-dep'd).

During the preparation of this work, the author used Claude Opus 4.7.

subjects.term: 11M26 (Nonreal zeros of zeta and L-functions; Riemann and other hypotheses)
